title: zingiber fallax
intro: Zingiber fallax, also known as false ginger, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the ginger family (Zingiberaceae) and is native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Zingiber fallax include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce nausea and vomiting, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Zingiber fallax can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as dried roots, teas, and infusions, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues.
intro_study: According to the study, zingiber fallax benefits include significantly improving body weight, fasting blood glucose, glycosylated hemoglobin 1c, serum insulin, homeostasis model assessment insulin resistance, high density lipoprotein-cholesterol, triglyceride, diastolic blood pressure, C-reactive protein, tumor necrosis factor-α, and interleukin 6, indicating its potential as an effective adjunctive therapy in the management of type 2 diabetes mellitus and prevention of cardiovascular complications.
